name: Chicken Enchiladas
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds chopped cooked chicken (up to 3 lbs)
1 4.5 oz can chopped mild green chilies
recipeInstructions:
Mexican Gravy:
Melt margarine in a saucepan. In a bowl, mix together dry ingredients: chili powder, flour, garlic salt, ground cumin, and crushed oregano. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the melted margarine while stirring constantly. The mixture will become crumbly. Slowly add chicken broth to the margarine mixture, stirring constantly. Stir in tomato sauce.
Enchiladas:
In a mixing bowl, stir together chicken, chopped green chilies, onion, and 1 cup of Mexican gravy.
In the Crock-Pot, place foil handles (see directions below). Dip each tortilla into the Mexican gravy and lay them in the Crock-Pot. Spread about 3 tablespoons of chicken filling over each tortilla, and sprinkle with cheese and olives. Continue layering until the top of the Crock-Pot is reached. The final layer should be cheese and olive layer. Pour any excess gravy over the tortilla stack. Cook on Low for 4 to 6 hours or on High for 1 1/2 to 2 1/2 hours.
Foil Handles:
Tear off three 18 x 2-inch strips of heavy foil or use regular foil folded to double thickness. Criss-cross the foil strips in a spoke design and place them in the Crock-Pot to make lifting the tortilla stack easier.
